text: Channel and spatial attention mechanisms that significantly improve the discriminability of feature representation have demonstrated excellent performance in numerous computer vision tasks. However, channel dimension reduction for cross-channel modeling is not free from cost. In this paper, we design a new efficient multi-scale attention (EMA) module by focusing on two main aspects: 1) per channel information preservation and 2) the reduction of computational complexity. Our proposed module achieves the above two aspects by reshaping the partially channels to batch dimensions and grouping channel dimensions into sub-features with multiple scales to ensure that spatial semantic features are well distributed inside each group of features. Moreover, in the proposed EMA module, in addition to the global information used for re-calibrating the channel-wise weight in each parallel branch, the cross-dimension interaction is performed at the output feature of the two parallel branches to capture the pixel-level pairwise relationship between features. Finally, a large number of ablation studies and experiments are conducted on image classification and object detection tasks over various popular benchmarks, such as CIFAR-100, ImageNet-1k, MS COCO and VisDrone2019, to evaluate the performance of our proposed module.
